why_detail: Parser yields 2,893 free-text claim rows and 791 structured focal-test rows with D/N; representative/focal claims from journal papers, but not guaranteed declared headline treatment result.
Peer-reviewed JAP/Personnel Psychology correlation rows; useful D/N article corpus but not treatment/main-claim effects.
Parser yields 1,969 original-claim rows across 762 DOI units; rows are replication targets, usually focal claims, but not broad journal sampling and not always treatments.
Random psychology-paper sample with coded key-question effects from non-preregistered articles; public rows include D/N but not DOI/title metadata.
Published-study rows from meta-analyses; no direct journal-paper main-result flag.
Sampled social/personality journal articles with coded critical hypothesized tests; useful focal-result corpus but some papers contribute multiple study-level tests.
Random-sample psychology article corpus with one coded main-question result per paper and D/N recovered from the public SAV.

Checks every row already plotted in the replication-pair figure and adds original-side D/N estimates only when the original DOI/title is not already represented in Plot 2; source is replication-target originals rather than broad journal sampling.
Published trial reports linked to ClinicalTrials.gov primary outcomes through the local CliniFact bridge; conservative proxy for journal-side primary-result D/N.
Published Swedish social-intervention effectiveness articles; primary-outcome flags are used for plotted rows and nonprimary rows remain comparator only.

status_label: causal-test corpus but not main-result selected
why_detail: Rows are RCT/IV/DID table test statistics from published economics articles with N; no flag for the paper's main result.
Has D/N and peer_reviewed metadata, but effect rows are selected through meta-analytic databases rather than direct paper main-result extraction.
Sampled political-science articles with author-coded main findings; parser keeps the authors' model==1 and number_of_dv==1 restriction.
Random psychology-paper sample with author-coded focal effects; focal rows are plotted while random-effect rows are retained as comparator only.
Preregistered psychology article sample from the same public corpus; key-question effects are D/N-ready and plotted separately from the non-preregistered sample.
Rows are intervention-comparator-outcome numerical RCT extractions from PMC full text; no primary-outcome flag, but Evidence Inference/SPIRIT metadata could support a heuristic selector.
Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction.

status_label: treatment-arm selected but not primary-outcome flagged
why_detail: Rows are intervention/treatment-arm effects with outcome and arm fields; mostsig can identify the most significant arm, not necessarily the main prespecified result.
Rows are source estimates curated for meta-analyses; some publication flags exist, but not paper-main-result flags.
Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction.
Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction.
Rows are trial antidepressant efficacy effects; journal rows can be compared to FDA/regulatory rows.
Original published-paper effects selected for replication; close to key-claim effects but small and replication-project selected rather than broad journal-sampled.
Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction.
Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction.
Source estimates have publication flags and observation counts, but rows are curated through meta-analysis datasets rather than direct journal main-result extraction.
All extractable APA test statistics from journal articles; no primary/main/treatment-result flag.

The finer-grained ClinicalTrials.gov results extraction has primary registry-result D/N proxies for 7,722 trial/outcome units. It is not included because Plot 2 is a published-paper endpoint plot, while these rows are registry results rather than journal-published article endpoints.
Paper-linked curated genome-wide-significant association hits; not sampled article-level journal treatment/results rows.
344 major cardiovascular RCTs with primary endpoints and 263 superiority effect-size rows reported by article; no public row-level CSV located.

status_label: random-effect comparator from an included source
why_detail: The Linden random-effect rows cover the same 158 sampled psychology papers as the included focal-effect rows, but they are deliberately non-focal within-paper comparator effects. Plot 2 keeps the author-coded focal effects and excludes these random-effect counterparts.

Public Aczel et al. null-hypothesis support audit yields 62 usable t-test D/N rows across 30 paper units after applying the authors' strict analyzable-subset rule. It is not included because the selection rule targets negative or nonsignificant claims, not focal published-paper endpoints.
Published journal test-statistic corpus; no decoded paper grouping yet and no primary/main-result flag.
Potential large medical proxy: primary registry outcome rows linked to PMIDs; not journal-extracted article results and registry-publication disagreements are expected.
Rows come through peer-reviewed meta-analyses; underlying source rows may be journals, working papers, or reports and no main-result flag exists.
